markdown:
#  Set a Default Funding Account as a Payor 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
##  Background 
With Procore Pay, your company's Payments Admin can add one or multiple funding accounts in the payor environment. A _funding account_ is the bank account from which the drawdown funding is requested to pay a disbursement. A company can have multiple funding accounts. A company can designate one (1) of those accounts to be your company's default funding account for new disbursements. A Payments Admin can change the default designation.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)
  * **Additional Information** :
    * Accounts must be in the 'Verified' status to be set as the default funding account.
    * The first 'Verified' funding account is automatically set as the default funding account for a business entity.
    * Before deactivating the default funding account, you must use the steps below to designate another account as the default.


##  Prerequisites 
  * [Add Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/add-funding-accounts)


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the Company level **Payments** tool.
  2. Click the **Payments Settings** ![Icons Settings Gear](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/8d4e4403c0eed73423d93c414c08e3a6/35b17d2e5497c876221891c1d9e5a114/icons-settings-gear.png?fm=webp&h=15) icon.  
This opens the Payments Settings page. The 'Business Entities' page in the 'Payments Processing' tab is active by default. This page lists each Business Entity configured to pay invoices in your company's Procore Pay software.
  3. Locate the business entity to modify and click its funding account(s) link.  
![Funding Account Link](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/c3e91c1c77f780cc9e083b499b4fcfc4/5c8d6a36ef3310ef98a587256e89486c/funding-account-link.png?fm=webp)
  4. Locate the account to modify.
  5. Click the **Overflow** menu and choose **Set as Default Funding Account**.  
  
![Set As Default Funding Account](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/ac66a4b98dab0cb332fb6c0f4d2308c5/d010cdaaa0565306c3047e49004cc05b/set-as-default-funding-account.png?fm=webp)  
  
The account is marked _Default_ and moves to the top of the entity's Funding Accounts list.   
![Default Funding Account Marked](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/163f36f97e19220dc5ee4106e3111cef/d951b0f24ac8c30be74fff93b80f96dc/default-funding-account-marked.png?fm=webp)
#####  Tip
**Can I deactivate our default account at a later time?** No. The default account cannot be deactivated in Procore. Before you can deactivate an account, you must remove the default designation. See [Deactivate Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/deactivate-accounts).


## See Also
  * [View Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/view-funding-accounts-as-a-payor)
  * [Deactivate Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/deactivate-funding-accounts-as-a-payor)
  * [Configure Payment Processing as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/configure-payment-processing-as-a-payor)


In Procore Pay, a business entity is an organization recognized as separate from its owner(s). Some construction companies operate as a single business entity, while others operate as multiple business entities, with each entity, subsidiary, or division focusing on overseeing a different market or sector.
title: Set a Default Funding Account as a Payor
description: How to set the default funding account for Procore Pay as a Payments Admin.
